# Topic 4 capstone

We bring together the chart types and customisation tools from this topic to answer a single research question — producing two complementary publication-ready figures.

{{< video https://vimeo.com/1196320474/2d00e509c3?fl=ml&fe=ec >}}

```python
# Research question: How has the relationship between wealth and
# health changed globally since 1952, and does it differ by continent?

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

plt.style.use("seaborn-v0_8-whitegrid")

gapminder = pd.read_csv("data/gapminder.csv")

# Consistent colour palette for continents across all charts
COLOURS = {
    "Africa":   "#E07B54",
    "Americas": "#5B8DB8",
    "Asia":     "#7AAF6E",
    "Europe":   "#9B6DB5",
    "Oceania":  "#E8B84B",
}

# ── Chart 1: Life expectancy over time by continent ──────────

continent_life = gapminder.groupby(["year", "continent"])["lifeExp"].mean().reset_index()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))

for continent in continent_life["continent"].unique():
    group = continent_life[continent_life["continent"] == continent]
    ax.plot(group["year"], group["lifeExp"],
            label=continent, color=COLOURS[continent],
            linewidth=2, marker="o", markersize=4)

ax.set_xlabel("Year", fontsize=11)
ax.set_ylabel("Mean life expectancy (years)", fontsize=11)
ax.set_title("Life expectancy over time by continent",
             fontsize=13, fontweight="bold")
ax.legend(title="Continent")

plt.tight_layout()
fig.savefig("output/01_life_expectancy_trend.png",       # save before show
            dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
plt.close()

# ── Chart 2: Wealth vs health in 1952 and 2007 ──────────────

fig, axes = plt.subplots(nrows=1, ncols=2, figsize=(14, 6))

# Left panel: 1952
gap_1952 = gapminder[gapminder["year"] == 1952]

for continent in gap_1952["continent"].unique():
    group = gap_1952[gap_1952["continent"] == continent]
    axes[0].scatter(group["gdpPercap"], group["lifeExp"],
                    s=group["pop"] / 1_000_000,
                    color=COLOURS[continent],
                    label=continent, alpha=0.7)

axes[0].set_title("1952", fontsize=13, fontweight="bold")

# Right panel: 2007
gap_2007 = gapminder[gapminder["year"] == 2007]

for continent in gap_2007["continent"].unique():
    group = gap_2007[gap_2007["continent"] == continent]
    axes[1].scatter(group["gdpPercap"], group["lifeExp"],
                    s=group["pop"] / 1_000_000,
                    color=COLOURS[continent],
                    label=continent, alpha=0.7)

axes[1].set_title("2007", fontsize=13, fontweight="bold")

# Apply shared formatting to both panels
for i in range(2):
    axes[i].set_xscale("log")
    axes[i].set_xlabel("GDP per capita (USD, log scale)")
    axes[i].set_ylabel("Life expectancy (years)")
    axes[i].set_ylim(20, 90)
    axes[i].legend()

fig.suptitle("GDP per capita vs life expectancy (bubble size = population)",
             fontsize=13, fontweight="bold")

plt.tight_layout()
fig.savefig("output/02_wealth_health_scatter.png",
            dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
plt.close()
```
